name: Restore The Grouped-Row Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling Surface

summary.name: Restore The Grouped-Row Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling Surface
summary.hypothesis: Round 25 gave the structural answer we needed from the auxiliary 256x128 family: the branch stayed correct, but runtime regressed to 24.690687 ms and the machine state stayed trapped at 48.23% tensor activity and 16.57% warps active. That is enough evidence to stop spending more immediate budget on this family. The best next move is therefore the strongest correctness-proven alternate surface already sitting in the frontier: the grouped-row non-PTX 128x128 sibling. It previously measured 24.178688 ms, which is inside the accepted-base noise band, while still replacing the PTX microkernel-specific control and export surface. That makes it the best round-26 recommendation: it recovers most of the round-25 loss without collapsing straight back into another same-surface PTX restore.
summary.expected_bottleneck: PTX-microkernel-specific control and export coupling on the current winner surface, while preserving grouped-row locality and the same broad 128x128 footprint.
summary.risk: Low to medium. This is an exact restore of a measured-correct alternate surface, but its prior machine state was still plateau-like, so the main risk is spending one round to recover correctness and comparability without opening a new headroom signal.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases

score_breakdown.formula: rank_score + frontier_leader_bonus + measured_recovery_bonus + alternate_surface_bonus + user_guidance_bonus - risk_penalty

ranking_notes: This now ranks first because it is the top frontier family representative after the auxiliary 256x128 miss.
It gives back almost all of the round-25 regression while still keeping the search on a genuinely different execution surface from the PTX winner.
It is more aligned with the current policy than another frozen PTX micro-family replay.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the sibling surface no longer lands inside the 24.16-24.19 ms band or if it reproduces the same plateau signature without any execution-surface advantage worth preserving.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
